title: Why is the bowl ninety now?
summary: A regular customer asks the pho chef why the bowl is suddenly twenty thousand more. The chef sits down and walks through the numbers.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Excuse me. Why is the bowl of pho now ninety thousand? Last month it was seventy.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Yes. I raised the price two weeks ago.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Twenty thousand more. That is a big jump.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: I know. Can I sit with you for a minute? I want to explain.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Beef. My beef costs twice as much this year. The farmer who used to supply me retired. The new supplier is twenty per cent more expensive.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: I tried five. Three were cheaper, but the meat was not the same. Two were the same quality but more expensive. I chose what tastes best.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Vegetables are slightly more. Rice noodles, the same as last year.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Mostly. Also the gas. The kitchen used to cost me two million a month. Now it is two and a half.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Why did you not raise the price earlier?

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: I waited. I did not want to. Last week I added the numbers honestly. I lost money for six months. I cannot lose money any longer.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Some customers will not come back. I understand. The bowl is still the same bowl. I do not want to make it smaller. I do not want to use cheaper beef. So the price goes up.

body.speaker: Chef
body.text: Thank you. Sit. Your bowl is on me today.

grammarNotes.title: 'Used to' for a past habit or state that has ended
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'used to + động từ nguyên thể' để nói về một thói quen hoặc trạng thái trong quá khứ mà nay không còn. Trong bài: 'The farmer who used to supply me retired.' (Người nông dân từng cung cấp cho tôi đã nghỉ hưu.) / 'The kitchen used to cost me two million a month.' Hàm ý: bây giờ không còn vậy nữa.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'used to + base verb' for a past habit or state that no longer happens. From the dialogue: 'The farmer who used to supply me retired.' / 'The kitchen used to cost me two million a month.' The form implies that the situation has now changed.

cloze.template:
Customer: Excuse me. Why is the bowl of pho now {{b1}} thousand? Last month it was {{b2}}.
Chef: Yes. I {{b3}} the price two weeks ago.
Customer: Twenty thousand more. That is a big jump.
Chef: I know. Can I sit with you for a minute? I want to {{b4}}.

Chef: {{b5}}. My beef costs twice as much this year. The farmer who used to supply me {{b6}}. The new supplier is twenty per cent more expensive.
Customer: Could you find another?
Chef: I tried {{b7}}. Three were cheaper, but the meat was not the same. Two were the same quality but more expensive. I chose what tastes best.
Customer: And {{b8}}?
Chef: Vegetables are slightly more. Rice {{b9}}, the same as last year.
Customer: So it is the beef.
Chef: Mostly. Also the {{b10}}. The kitchen used to cost me two million a month. Now it is two and a half.
Customer: Why did you not raise the price earlier?

Customer: I will keep coming.
Chef: Thank you. Sit. Your bowl is on me today.

blanks.explanation: The bowl is now ninety thousand.
Last month it was seventy thousand.
He raised the price two weeks ago.
He wants to explain — not argue.
Beef is the main cost driver.
The farmer who used to supply him retired.
The customer asks about vegetables next.
Rice noodles — the same price as last year.
Also the gas — the kitchen's monthly cost has gone up.
He lost money for six months before raising the price.
He does not want to use cheaper beef — that would reduce quality.

translationVi:
Khách: Anh ơi, sao tô phở giờ chín mươi nghìn ạ? Tháng trước bảy mươi mà.
Đầu bếp: Đúng rồi. Tôi tăng giá hai tuần trước.
Khách: Tăng hai mươi nghìn. Nhảy nhiều thế.
Đầu bếp: Tôi biết. Tôi ngồi cùng chị một phút được không? Tôi muốn giải thích.
Khách: Anh nói đi.
Đầu bếp: Là thịt bò. Bò năm nay đắt gấp đôi. Người nông dân từng cung cấp cho tôi đã nghỉ. Nhà cung cấp mới đắt hơn hai mươi phần trăm.
Khách: Anh không tìm chỗ khác được sao?
Đầu bếp: Tôi đã thử năm chỗ. Ba chỗ rẻ hơn, nhưng thịt không cùng chất lượng. Hai chỗ cùng chất lượng nhưng đắt hơn. Tôi chọn cái có vị ngon nhất.
Khách: Còn rau thì sao?
Đầu bếp: Rau hơi đắt hơn một chút. Bánh phở thì y như năm ngoái.
Khách: Vậy là do thịt bò.
Đầu bếp: Phần lớn. Cả gas nữa. Trước bếp tốn của tôi hai triệu một tháng. Giờ là hai triệu rưỡi.
Khách: Sao anh không tăng sớm hơn?
Đầu bếp: Tôi cố đợi. Tôi không muốn. Tuần trước tôi cộng các con số một cách thật thà. Tôi đã lỗ trong sáu tháng. Tôi không thể lỗ thêm nữa.
Khách: Hợp lý mà.
Đầu bếp: Có khách sẽ không quay lại. Tôi hiểu. Tô vẫn là tô như cũ. Tôi không muốn làm tô nhỏ lại. Tôi không muốn dùng thịt bò rẻ hơn. Nên giá phải lên.
Khách: Tôi vẫn sẽ tới ăn.
Đầu bếp: Cảm ơn chị. Mời chị ngồi. Tô của chị hôm nay tôi mời.

questions.prompt: What was the price last month, and what is it now?
questions.explanation: 'Last month it was seventy.' / 'Why is the bowl of pho now ninety thousand?'

questions.prompt: Why does the chef ask to sit with the customer?
questions.hint: Look at the chef's first long turn.

questions.prompt: What is the main reason for the price rise?
options: Beef — costs twice as much; new supplier is 20% more expensive
questions.explanation: 'My beef costs twice as much this year.'
questions.hint: Look at the chef's reply to 'Please'.

questions.prompt: How many alternative suppliers did the chef try?
questions.hint: Look at the chef's reply about other suppliers.

questions.prompt: Why didn't he keep one of the cheaper ones?
options: The meat was not the same — quality was lower
questions.explanation: 'Three were cheaper, but the meat was not the same.'

questions.prompt: Apart from beef, what else has gone up?
questions.explanation: 'Vegetables are slightly more.' / 'Also the gas. The kitchen used to cost me two million a month. Now it is two and a half.'
questions.hint: Look at the chef's later replies.

questions.prompt: Why didn't the chef raise the price earlier?
options: He waited; he did not want to
He thought beef would get cheaper
questions.explanation: 'I waited. I did not want to.'
questions.hint: Look at the chef's reply about the timing.

questions.prompt: How long had he been losing money?
What does the chef refuse to do, even at a higher cost?
options: Make the bowl smaller or use cheaper beef
questions.explanation: 'I do not want to make it smaller. I do not want to use cheaper beef.'
questions.hint: Look at his explanation near the end.

options: The customer says she will keep coming; the chef offers her today's bowl on the house
questions.explanation: 'I will keep coming.' / 'Sit. Your bowl is on me today.'
questions.hint: Look at the last two turns.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The chef says, 'The bowl is still the same bowl.' Think of a small thing you buy regularly. If its price went up, would you rather have a smaller version at the old price, or the same thing at the higher price? Why?
